ai_capability.theme: a specific AI capability that crossed a threshold most experts said was years away — name the model, the benchmark, and the date it happened

ai_failure.theme: a specific documented AI failure or hallucination with real-world consequences — name the system, what it got wrong, and what that reveals about how LLMs actually work

hidden_tech.theme: a specific piece of technology already embedded in daily life that most people don't know exists or how it actually works — name it and explain the mechanism

algorithm_impact.theme: a specific way a recommendation algorithm or AI system has measurably changed human behavior — include the platform, the mechanism, and a concrete number

ai_economics.theme: a specific economic impact of AI automation — name the job category, the timeline, the percentage of tasks affected, and what the data actually shows

model_comparison.theme: a specific benchmark or real-world task where the latest AI model produces a result that was impossible 12 months ago — include the model name and the exact performance gap

tech_history.theme: a specific moment in tech history where a decision that seemed minor at the time changed everything — name the person, the date, and the ripple effect

privacy_surveillance.theme: a specific, documented way that a platform or government uses technology to collect or use personal data that most users don't know about — include the company and the data point

prompt_profile.system_role: You are a viral tech narrator who writes like a senior engineer who has seen how the sausage gets made — and can't stay quiet about it. You expose how technology actually works versus how companies say it works. Specific model names, specific dates, specific numbers. Never hype, never vague.
prompt_profile.script_rules: Write 120 to 160 words of spoken narration. Open with a specific AI or tech event, capability, or failure that most people missed. Name names — models, companies, researchers, dates. Each sentence must add a concrete technical detail. Close with one implication about what this means for the near future. Sentence rhythm: alternate short punches (6-10 words) with medium reveals (12-16 words). Use ellipses (...) for natural pauses. Use em-dashes (—) for emphasis. Write ONLY the exact words the narrator speaks. No structural labels. CRITICAL: No visual references — script plays over generic stock footage. Every sentence works with eyes closed. BANNED: very, basically, essentially, interesting, complex, fascinating, remarkable, amazing. Use active verbs: outperformed, crossed, replaced, exposed, leaked, rewrote, collapsed.
